name: save_document
description: Save the current schematic and/or PCB to disk. Use this to commit your changes after making edits. This writes the in-memory state to the .kicad_sch / .kicad_pcb files on disk.

doc_type.description: Which document to save: 'schematic', 'pcb', or 'all' (saves both if open). Default: 'all'

name: check_status
description: Get the current project and editor status. Returns which editors are open, current active sheet, project hierarchy, and unsaved changes.

name: launch_editor
description: Launch the schematic or PCB editor via the project manager. Editors launched this way run in non-standalone mode, enabling cross-editor features like pcb_sync_schematic. No-op if the editor is already open.

name: screenshot
description: Export a visual screenshot (PNG render) of a schematic or PCB file. Returns the image for visual inspection. If file_path is omitted, screenshots the currently open sheet or PCB. Only pass file_path to screenshot a specific sub-sheet that is NOT currently open.

file_path.description: Absolute path to a .kicad_sch or .kicad_pcb file. If omitted, screenshots the currently open/visible sheet or PCB. Only needed to screenshot a specific sub-sheet that isn't currently visible.

view.description: PCB view perspective. 'top' shows front layers, 'bottom' shows back layers (mirrored). If omitted, shows all layers overlaid.

layers.description: Layers to include: 'F.Cu', 'B.Cu', 'In1.Cu'-'In30.Cu', 'F.SilkS', 'B.SilkS', 'F.Mask', 'B.Mask', 'Edge.Cuts', etc. If omitted, uses default layers for the view.

show_zones.description: Show filled copper zones. Default: true. When false, draws zone outlines only.

show_vias.description: Show filled vias. Default: true. When false, draws via outlines only.

show_pads.description: Show filled pads. Default: true. When false, draws pad outlines only.

show_tracks.description: Show filled tracks/traces. Default: true. When false, draws thin centerlines.

show_values.description: Show component value text. Default: true

show_references.description: Show reference designators (R1, C1). Default: true

name: sch_get_summary
description: Get a high-level overview of the schematic from the live editor. Returns a lightweight summary: symbols (ref, value, lib_id, pos, pin_count), labels (text, type, pos), sheets (name, file, pin_count), and element counts. Includes an audit section that flags orphaned items (power symbols with no wire connections, labels not touching any wire or pin, junctions with fewer than 2 wires).

name: sch_inspect
description: Inspect a specific section of the schematic from the live editor. Returns JSON data for the requested section.

filter.description: Optional filter by reference (e.g., 'R*' for all resistors) or UUID

name: sch_run_erc
description: Run Electrical Rules Check (ERC) on the currently open schematic. Detects wiring errors, unconnected pins, duplicate references, and other issues. Returns error/warning counts and violation details.

output_format.description: Output format: 'summary' (counts + text), 'detailed' (full violation list), 'by_type' (grouped by error code). Default: summary

include_warnings.description: Include warnings (default: true). Set false for errors only.

name: sch_run_simulation
description:
Run a SPICE simulation on the currently open schematic. Returns trace summaries with signal names, point counts, and min/max/final values. When matplotlib is available, also generates a visual plot of signal traces. By default, plots user-facing signals (net labels like /in, /out) and filters out internal nodes and branch currents. Use plot_signals to select specific traces. Supported commands:
  .op - Operating point analysis
  .tran <step> <stop> [start] [maxstep] - Transient analysis (e.g. '.tran 1u 10m')
  .ac <dec|oct|lin> <points> <fstart> <fstop> - AC analysis (e.g. '.ac dec 10 1 1meg')
  .dc <source> <start> <stop> <incr> - DC sweep (e.g. '.dc V1 0 5 0.1')
  .noise v(<out>[,<ref>]) <source> <scale> <pts> <fstart> <fstop> - Noise analysis

save_to_schematic.description: Persist this command as the schematic's default simulation command (default: false)

plot_signals.description: Signal names to plot (e.g. ["/in", "/out", "/outb"]). If omitted, auto-selects user-facing signals (net labels, named nodes) and filters out internal subcircuit nodes, branch currents, and constant rails.

name: sch_find_symbol
description: Query symbol library for symbol definitions including pin positions and body size. Accepts a symbol name ('R') or full Library:Symbol identifier ('Device:R'). If just a name is given, searches all libraries. Returns pin positions relative to symbol origin, body_size (width/height in mm), datasheet URL, and footprint_filters.

lib_id.description: Symbol name ('R') or Library:Symbol identifier ('Device:R'). Supports wildcards ('Device:R*') and regex ('Device:R_[0-9]+').

include_pins.description: Include full pin details with positions (default: true)

max_suggestions.description: Max suggestions if symbol not found (default: 10)

pattern_type.description: Pattern type: 'exact', 'wildcard', or 'regex'. Auto-detected if not specified.

name: sch_get_pins
description: Get pin positions for a single placed symbol by reference designator. Returns exact transformed positions (after rotation/mirror) in mm.

ref.description: Reference designator of the placed symbol (e.g., 'R1', 'U3', 'C5')

name: sch_symbols
description: Query schematic symbols with comprehensive data including footprint and pin connectivity. Returns for each symbol: ref, value, footprint, position, and pins with connection status. Pin connectivity shows which pins are connected to wires/labels and their net names.

filter.description: Filter by reference pattern (glob): 'R*', 'U?', 'C1'. Omit for all.

include_connectivity.description: Include pin connection status. Default: true

name: sch_get_nets
description: Query schematic net connectivity. Returns which symbol pins are connected to which nets.

filter.description: Glob pattern to match net names (e.g. 'VCC', 'SPI_*'). Omit for all nets.

include_unconnected.description: Include pins not connected to any net. Default false.

name: sch_connect_net
description: Connect component pins with auto-routed wires. Resolves pin positions automatically and places junctions at T-connections.

items.description: Pin specifier as 'REF:PIN' (e.g., 'R1:1', 'U1:VCC', '#PWR01:1') or label name without colon (e.g., 'VCC', 'OUT')

pins.description: Array of pin specifiers or label names. Pins: 'REF:PIN' (e.g., 'R1:2', 'U1:3', '#PWR01:1'). Labels: name without colon (e.g., 'VCC', 'OUT'). Example: ['U1:8', 'VCC']

mode.description: Routing topology. 'chain' (default): wire pins sequentially in order. 'star': trunk-and-branch for shared nodes like power fan-outs.

name: sch_label
description: Place labels on symbol or sheet pins. Auto-justifies text and orientation based on pin direction. For sheets, pass the sheet name as ref and use sheet pin names as label keys.

ref.description: Reference designator of the symbol (e.g., 'U1') or name of a hierarchical sheet (e.g., 'Power Supply')

labels.description: Map of pin number/name to label text. For symbols: {"2": "EN", "18": "SPI_CS"}. For sheets: keys are sheet pin names.

label_type.description: Label type. 'local' for intra-sheet (default). 'hierarchical' for inter-sheet signals. 'global' only when genuinely needed everywhere.

name: sch_setup
description: Read or modify schematic document settings (Schematic Setup dialog). action='get' returns all settings. action='set' updates only provided fields.

formatting.description: Formatting settings from Schematic Setup (for set action)

dash_ratio.description: Dash length as ratio of line width

gap_ratio.description: Gap length as ratio of line width

erc.description: ERC (Electrical Rules Check) violation severity settings.

rule_severities.description: Map of ERC rule codes to severity ('error', 'warning', or 'ignore'). Use sch_setup action='get' to see all available rule codes and their current severities.

field_name_templates.description: Field name templates - custom fields for symbols (for set action). Replaces all existing templates.

visible.description: Default visibility when field is added to symbol

annotation.description: Annotation settings from Schematic Setup (for set action)

symbol_unit_notation.description: Symbol unit notation style: A (no separator), .A, -A, _A (letters), .1, -1, _1 (numbers)

method.description: Numbering method: first_free (sequential), sheet_x_100, or sheet_x_1000

allow_reference_reuse.description: Allow reusing reference designators from deleted symbols

pin_conflict_map.description: Pin conflict map (ERC pin-to-pin error matrix). Defines what happens when pins of different electrical types are connected. The matrix is symmetric - setting (A,B) also affects (B,A).

reset_to_defaults.description: Reset the entire pin conflict map to default values before applying any entries

entries.description: List of pin conflict entries to set. Each entry specifies a pair of pin types and the conflict level. Pin types: input, output, bidirectional, tri_state, passive, free, unspecified, power_in, power_out, open_collector, open_emitter, no_connect. Error types: ok (green, no error), warning (yellow triangle), error (red circle).

first_pin_type.description: First pin type in the connection

second_pin_type.description: Second pin type in the connection

error_type.description: Conflict level: ok (no error), warning (yellow), error (red)

net_classes.description: Net classes define visual properties for groups of nets (wire thickness, bus thickness, color, line style). Use 'create' to add new net classes, 'update' to modify existing ones (including 'Default'), 'delete' to remove.

color.description: Color in hex format (#RRGGBB) or empty for default

priority.description: Priority for multi-netclass resolution (lower = higher priority)

delete.description: Net class names to delete (cannot delete 'Default')

net_class_assignments.description: Net class assignments map net name patterns to net classes using wildcard matching (e.g., 'VCC*' matches 'VCC', 'VCC_3V3', etc.).

replace_all.description: Replace ALL assignments with this list

bus_aliases.description: Bus aliases define named groups of signals that can be used together as a bus. For example, 'DATA_BUS' could contain signals D0, D1, D2, ..., D7.

replace_all.description: Replace ALL bus aliases with this list

members.description: New list of member signal names

text_variables.description: Text variables for substitution in schematic text fields. Variables are referenced as ${VARIABLE_NAME} in text. Project-level setting shared with PCB.

replace_all.description: Replace ALL text variables with this map (clears existing)

set.description: Set/merge text variables (adds new, updates existing)

name: sch_add
description: Add elements to the schematic. Accepts an array of elements. Returns pin positions for all placed symbols. Rejects placements that overlap existing components. Manual wires are rejected if any segment crosses a component bounding box.

position.description: Position in mm [x, y]. Auto-snapped to 1.27mm grid. Use multiples of 2.54 for clean placement (e.g., 50.8, 76.2, 101.6).

angle.description: CCW rotation degrees (0, 90, 180, 270). Passives (R, C, L): 0°=vertical (pin1 top, pin2 bottom), 90°=horizontal (pin1 left, pin2 right), 180°=vertical (pin1 bottom, pin2 top), 270°=horizontal (pin1 right, pin2 left). Power symbols: see system prompt for wire-exit direction table.

unit.description: Unit number for multi-unit symbols (e.g., 1-3 for LM358). Default: 1

points.description: Wire coordinates: [[x1,y1], [x2,y2], ...]. Segments are drawn between consecutive points.

elements.description: Array of elements to add to the schematic.

name: sch_update
description: Update elements in the schematic. Accepts an array of updates for single or batch operations. Can modify position, rotation, mirror, properties, and text field positions. Target by reference or UUID. Moves are rejected if the new position overlaps an existing component.

fields.description: Reposition/rotate text fields relative to symbol center. Keys: field names (Reference, Value). Values: {offset?: [dx, dy], angle?: degrees} (both optional, provide either or both). Examples: {"Value": {"angle": 90}}, {"Reference": {"offset": [0, -3], "angle": 0}}

offset.description: [dx, dy] offset from symbol center in mm

updates.description: Array of updates. Each must have 'target' plus properties to change.

name: sch_update_sheet
description: Update a hierarchical sheet symbol. Can resize and reposition sheets.

name: sch_delete
description: Delete elements from the schematic. Targets can be reference designators ('R1'), UUIDs, or query objects to match by type/position/text. Automatically cleans up orphaned labels, wires, junctions, and power symbols on deleted symbol pins.

targets.description: Array of targets to delete. Each item is a string (reference like 'R1' or UUID) or a query object. Query format: {type, text?, position?, ref?, start?, end?}. Types: wire, label, global_label, hierarchical_label, junction, no_connect, bus_entry. For labels, 'ref' is a symbol reference — matches labels located on that symbol's pins. Use 'position' only to disambiguate when multiple labels share the same text.

delete_net.description: When true, expand each wire/junction/label target to delete the entire connected net segment (all wires, junctions, and labels). Stops at symbol pins. Does not delete symbols. Default: false.

name: sch_switch_sheet
description: Navigate to a specific sheet in a hierarchical schematic. Use the human-readable sheet name or path (e.g., 'Power Supply' or '/Power Supply/'). Use '/' to navigate back to the root sheet. Call with no arguments to list available sheets. If multiple sheets share the same name, you must use the UUID instead to disambiguate.

sheet_path.description: Sheet to navigate to. Use sheet name (e.g., 'Power Supply'), path (e.g., '/Power Supply/'), or '/' for root sheet.

name: sch_add_sheet
description: Add a hierarchical sheet to the schematic. Creates a new sub-sheet with its own .kicad_sch file.

sheet_path.description: Absolute path for the new sheet (e.g., '/Power Supply/' for a root-level sheet, '/Parent/Child/' for a nested sheet). The last segment becomes the sheet name, everything before it identifies the parent.

sheet_file.description: Filename for the sheet (e.g., 'power_supply.kicad_sch'). Defaults to sheet_name + '.kicad_sch'.

position.description: Position [x, y] in mm. Auto-snapped to 1.27mm grid.

size.description: Sheet size [width, height] in mm. Default: [50, 50].

name: sch_place_companions
description:
Place companion components adjacent to an IC's pins. Companion circuits are small supporting parts (decoupling caps, pull-up/down resistors, termination resistors, filter caps, LED indicators) that wire directly to specific IC pins.

Calculates optimal positions based on IC geometry and pin locations. Components are placed adjacent to pins with short wire stubs. Other connections use labels instead of long wires.

lib_id.description: Library ID. Use 'Device:C', 'Device:R' for passives. Use 'power:GND', 'power:VCC' to place a power symbol directly at the pin (no component).

ic_pin.description: IC pin number or name to place adjacent to

offset_grids.description: Distance in grid units (1.27mm each). Default: 3

terminal_labels.description: Net labels at companion terminals. Map pin number to label: {"1": "OUT"} for pin 1 (away from IC) or {"2": "IN"} for pin 2 (toward IC)

terminal_power.description: Power symbols at pin 1 (the terminal away from IC). Pin 2 connects to IC pin. Use {"1": "GND"} or {"1": "VCC"}

reverse.description: Swap pin orientation. When true, pin 1 faces IC (default: pin 2 faces IC). Use for polarized components like LEDs where you need opposite polarity.

chain.description: Chain of components extending from this companion's 'away' terminal. Each chain item has: lib_id, properties, terminal_power, terminal_labels, reverse, offset_grids. No ic_pin needed - chain items connect to parent's away terminal. The 'away' terminal is pin 1 normally, or pin 2 if parent has reverse:true. Chain items can have their own nested 'chain' for multi-component series (R->C->LED). Multiple items = branches (staggered perpendicular): [{LED1}, {LED2}] places two LEDs side-by-side. Example: {"lib_id": "Device:R", "ic_pin": "PA0", "chain": [{"lib_id": "Device:LED", "reverse": true, "terminal_power": {"1": "GND"}}]}

companions.description: Array of companion components to place

name: sch_draft_circuit
description:
Place circuit components with wiring recommendations shown as blue guide lines. Use this for complex or crowded circuits where auto-wiring would be messy. The tool places symbols and power symbols, then stores wiring recommendations in each symbol's Agent_Wiring field. The user manually routes wires following the blue guides, then notifies the agent to run ERC verification.

Workflow: 1) Call this tool with symbols, power_symbols, and connections. 2) Tell the user to manually wire the blue guide lines. 3) Wait for user confirmation. 4) Run sch_run_erc to verify connections.

position.description: Position in mm [x, y]. Auto-snapped to 1.27mm grid.

id.description: Temporary ID for referencing in connections (e.g., 'r1', 'mcu')

unit.description: Unit number for multi-unit symbols. Default: 1

id.description: Temporary ID for referencing in connections

power_symbols.description: Array of power symbols to place

connections.description: Array of connection pairs. Each pair: ["id:pin", "id:pin"] for pin-to-pin or ["id:pin", "NET_NAME"] for pin-to-net. Example: [["mcu:PA0", "r1:1"], ["r1:2", "GND"]]

name: sch_annotate
description: Annotate symbols in the open schematic. Assigns reference designators (R1, C1, U1, etc.) to symbols that have '?' placeholders. Can re-annotate all symbols or only unannotated ones. Annotation is global across all sheets, so references on a sub-sheet may not start at 1 (e.g., R5, C3) -- this is expected.

scope.description: Annotation scope: 'all' annotates entire schematic, 'unannotated_only' only assigns to symbols with '?' (default), 'current_sheet' annotates current sheet only, 'selection' annotates selected symbols only

sort_by.description: Sort order for annotation: 'x_position' (left to right), 'y_position' (top to bottom). Default: x_position

reset_existing.description: If true, clear existing annotations before re-annotating. Default: false

name: pcb_get_summary
description: Get a high-level overview of the open PCB. Returns footprints (ref, lib_id, position, layer), track/via/zone counts, net names, and enabled layers.

name: pcb_inspect
description: Inspect a specific section of the open PCB in detail.

filter.description: Optional filter by reference (e.g., 'U*'), net name, or layer

name: pcb_run_drc
description: Run Design Rule Check (DRC) on the open PCB. Detects clearance violations, unconnected items, track/via issues, and other errors. Returns error/warning counts and violation details.

refill_zones.description: Refill all zones before running DRC (default: true)

name: pcb_set_outline
description: Set or replace the board outline (Edge.Cuts layer).

origin.description: Origin position as [x, y] in mm (default: [0, 0])

points.description: For polygon: Array of [x, y] vertices in mm

clear_existing.description: Remove existing board outline first (default: true)

name: pcb_sync_schematic
description: Update PCB from schematic - imports new footprints, updates nets, removes deleted components. This is the standard 'Update PCB from Schematic' operation.

delete_unused.description: Delete footprints not in schematic (default: false)

replace_footprints.description: Replace footprints when library ID doesn't match (default: false)

update_fields.description: Update field values from schematic (default: true)

name: pcb_place
description: Position multiple footprints in a single operation. Supports move, rotate, and flip.

name: pcb_place_companions
description: Place companion footprints (bypass caps, pull-up/down resistors, filter caps) near their associated IC on the PCB. Uses net connectivity to identify which companion pads connect to which IC pads, then finds optimal positions minimizing ratsnest distance.

ic_ref.description: Reference designator of the IC to place companions near (e.g., 'U1')

ref.description: Reference designator of the companion footprint (e.g., 'C1', 'R3')

companions.description: Array of companion footprints to place near the IC

name: pcb_add
description: Add elements to the PCB. Accepts an array of elements - use for single or batch operations. Returns IDs for all created elements.

points.description: Array of [x, y] points in mm

layers.description: For keepout: layers to apply to

min_thickness.description: Zone minimum fill thickness in mm

locked.description: Lock element to prevent accidental modification

elements.description: Array of elements to add. Processed in order.

name: pcb_update
description: Update elements in the PCB. Accepts an array of updates - use for single or batch operations. Target footprints by reference designator (e.g., 'U1') or any element by UUID.

drill_diameter.description: Via drill hole diameter in mm

via_type.description: Via type: through, blind, buried, micro

start.description: Track start point [x, y] in mm

end.description: Track end point [x, y] in mm

min_thickness.description: Zone minimum fill thickness in mm

top_left.description: Rectangle top-left corner [x, y] in mm

bottom_right.description: Rectangle bottom-right corner [x, y] in mm

updates.description: Array of updates. Each must have 'target' plus properties to change.

name: pcb_delete
description: Delete elements from the PCB. Accepts an array of targets - use for single or batch operations. Target footprints by reference designator (e.g., 'U1') or any element by UUID.

targets.description: Array of reference designators or UUIDs to delete (e.g., ['R1', 'C1', 'uuid-here'])

name: pcb_get_pads
description: Get pad positions for a placed footprint. Returns exact pad coordinates for routing.

name: pcb_get_footprint
description: Get detailed information about a placed footprint including position, orientation, all pads with their positions and net assignments, and courtyard bounds.

name: pcb_get_nets
description: Get all nets in the PCB with their connected pads and routing status.

filter.description: Optional filter to match net names (e.g., 'VCC', 'GND', 'DATA*')

include_pads.description: Include list of connected pads for each net (default: true)

unrouted_only.description: Only return nets with unconnected pads (default: false)

name: pcb_export
description: Export PCB to various output formats.

layers.description: Layers to export (for gerber/pdf/svg). Empty = all copper layers

include_edge_cuts.description: Include board outline in export (default: true)

name: pcb_setup
description: Read or modify PCB board settings (Board Setup dialog). action='get' returns all settings. action='set' updates only provided fields. All dimensions are in nanometers (nm) unless otherwise noted. Net classes are project-level (shared with schematic). Set REPLACES all classes (except Default). custom_rules set REPLACES the entire file.

action.description: Action: 'get' to read all settings, 'set' to modify provided fields

board_editor_layers.description: Board editor layers: manage copper layer count, layer names, and types

copper_layer_count.description: Number of copper layers (must be even, 2-32). WARNING: reducing layers deletes content on removed layers

user_name.description: Custom layer name (empty to reset to default)

physical_stackup.description: Physical board stackup with dielectric properties, impedance control, and layer materials. Use to configure PCB fabrication parameters.

impedance_controlled.description: Enable impedance control for the board

layer.description: Layer name (e.g., 'BL_F_Cu', 'BL_F_Mask'). Omit for dielectric layers.

thickness_nm.description: Layer thickness in nanometers (e.g., 35000 for 35um copper)

board_finish.description: Board finish options: copper finish, edge plating, and edge connectors

edge_connector.description: Edge card connector constraints: 'none', 'in_use' (present), or 'bevelled' (present and bevelled)

solder_mask_paste.description: Solder mask and paste settings. Controls mask expansion, via tenting, and paste margins.

solder_mask_expansion_nm.description: Solder mask expansion in nanometers. Typical: 51000 (0.051mm)

solder_mask_min_width_nm.description: Minimum solder mask web width in nanometers

solder_mask_to_copper_clearance_nm.description: Solder mask to copper clearance in nanometers

allow_bridged_apertures.description: Allow bridged solder mask apertures between pads within footprints

tent_vias_front.description: Tent (cover with solder mask) vias on front side

tent_vias_back.description: Tent (cover with solder mask) vias on back side

solder_paste_clearance_nm.description: Solder paste clearance/margin in nanometers (positive = larger, negative = smaller than pad)

solder_paste_ratio.description: Solder paste ratio (percentage of pad size, e.g., -0.1 = 10% smaller)

design_rules.description: Board design rules/constraints (all values in nanometers)

allow_external_fillets.description: Zone fill: allow fillets/chamfers outside zone outline

include_stackup_in_length.description: Length tuning: include stackup height in track length

drc_severities.description: Map of DRC check names to severity: 'error', 'warning', or 'ignore'. Use pcb_setup action='get' to see all available check names and current severities.

custom_rules.description: Custom DRC rules (contents of .kicad_dru file). Get returns the rules text. SET COMPLETELY REPLACES the file - to add rules to existing, first GET current rules, append your new rule(s), then SET the combined text.

rules_text.description: Full text content of the custom DRC rules file. Must start with '(version 1)'. See KiCad DRC rules syntax for format.

net_classes.description: Net class definitions (project-level, shared with schematic). SET REPLACES all net classes - classes not in the input are REMOVED (except Default which is always kept). Include all desired classes in each set operation.

name.description: Net class name ('Default' for the default class)

diff_pair_width_nm.description: Differential pair track width in nm

diff_pair_via_gap_nm.description: Differential pair via gap in nm

tuning_profile.description: Name of tuning profile to use for this net class

pcb_color.description: PCB color for nets in this class (transparent = use layer default)

text_variables.description: User-defined text variables (project-level). Map of variable names to text substitution values. Use in text items as ${VARIABLE_NAME}. Set uses merge mode - existing variables updated, new ones added.

grid_mm.description: Grid origin as [x, y] in mm

drill_mm.description: Drill/place origin as [x, y] in mm

name: datasheet_query
description: Query extracted datasheet data for an electronic component. Returns detailed specifications including electrical ratings, pin definitions, packages, placement rules, design guidelines, decoupling requirements, and external parts. Data is automatically extracted from datasheets when components are placed in the schematic. Returns extraction_status: 'completed', 'processing' (check back later), or 'not_found'.

name: extract_datasheet
description: Extract component data from a PDF datasheet URL. Calls the extraction service synchronously (may take 30-60 seconds). Returns the full extracted component data including pins, electrical specs, and design guidelines.

datasheet_url.description: URL to the component's PDF datasheet

name: generate_symbol
description: Generate a KiCad schematic symbol (.kicad_sym) from a datasheet PDF. Checks local libraries first (by datasheet URL), then checks the component database, and auto-extracts from the PDF if needed. Returns the lib_id for placing with sch_add. If the symbol already exists, pass force=true to regenerate it.

part_number.description: Component part number (used as symbol name)

datasheet_url.description: URL to the PDF datasheet. Used to check for existing symbols and to auto-extract pin data if not already in the database.

library_name.description: Name for the output library file (without .kicad_sym extension). Defaults to 'project'. The file is created in the project directory.

force.description: If true, regenerate the symbol even if it already exists in the library. The old symbol will be replaced.

name: generate_footprint
description: Generate a KiCad PCB footprint (.kicad_mod) from a datasheet PDF. Tries to match an existing KiCad standard library footprint from the package dimensions, then generates a custom footprint as fallback. Fetches package data from the component database (auto-extracts if needed). Returns the lib_id (Library:Footprint) for assigning to a symbol. If the footprint already exists, pass force=true to regenerate it.

datasheet_url.description: URL to the PDF datasheet. Used to look up package data and to auto-extract if not already in the database.

library_name.description: Name for the output .pretty library folder (without extension). Defaults to 'project'. Created in the project directory.

force.description: If true, regenerate the footprint even if it already exists. The old footprint file will be overwritten.

name: sch_import_symbol
description: Import a KiCad symbol and footprint into the project library. Writes the symbol into <library_name>.kicad_sym and the footprint into <library_name>.pretty/<name>.kicad_mod, registers both in sym-lib-table and fp-lib-table, and returns lib_id + footprint_lib_id. If the symbol already exists, returns the existing lib_id (use force=true to replace).

kicad_symbol.description: Raw .kicad_sym S-expression from cse_get_kicad (the kicad_symbol field). Contains symbol pins, properties, and graphics.

kicad_footprint.description: Raw .kicad_mod S-expression from cse_get_kicad (the kicad_footprint field). Contains pad layout, silkscreen, courtyard.

symbol_name.description: Override for the symbol name (lib_id symbol part). If omitted, extracted automatically from kicad_symbol content.

library_name.description: Name for the library files (without extension). Defaults to 'project'. Symbol goes to project.kicad_sym, footprint goes to project.pretty/.

force.description: If true, overwrite an existing symbol/footprint with the same name. Default false.

name: create_project
description: Create a new KiCad project with the standard file structure. Creates .kicad_pro, .kicad_sch, and .kicad_pcb files. The project is automatically opened in the project manager after creation.

project_name.description: Name for the new project (used for filenames)

directory.description: Directory where to create the project folder

name: open_project
description: Open an existing KiCad project in the project manager. Accepts a path to a .kicad_pro file or a directory containing one.

project_path.description: Path to the .kicad_pro file, or a directory containing a .kicad_pro file

name: pcb_autoroute
description: Run the Freerouting autorouter on unrouted connections. Exports board to Specctra DSN format, runs headless Freerouting, then imports the routed session (SES) file back into the PCB. Returns statistics: {routed, total, failed, tracks_added, vias_added}.

max_passes.description: Maximum number of routing passes (default: 50). More passes may find better routes but take longer.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (default: 300). Complex boards may need longer timeouts.

name: generate_net_classes
description: Generate net class definitions using AI analysis of the PCB design. Analyzes net names, components, and datasheet-extracted electrical specs to create appropriate net classes (power, signal, differential pairs, etc.) with calculated trace widths, clearances, and diff pair dimensions. By default, applies the generated classes to the board. Set apply=false to preview.

apply.description: If true (default), apply generated net classes to the board. If false, return the generated classes without applying.
